recentTweets.text: The only election interference happening in this country is coming straight from the White House, and no amount of pathetic late night rambling from a failing, fading president will change anyone’s mind

So yesterday, Jay Clayton:

-Attacked vote by mail
-Questioned U.S. elections integrity
-Refused to say Joe Biden won the 2020 election
-Refused to answer who asked him to subpoena NYT reporters

Clayton is unfit to serve as America’s top intelligence officer.

America is strongest when we move forward with our eyes open & a clear plan for a better future. Here’s what I heard last week from Eastern Oregon about that better future for forestry, health care & more: 
https://t.co/c1VXH43PF7

Couple reminders as Todd Blanche testifies on Capitol Hill today:

1. Trump fired Bondi because she failed to keep his name out of the Epstein files.

2. Todd Blanche was Trump’s personal attorney and is likely to go even further in protecting Trump from any Epstein fallout.

Don't worry we've got the receipts. 

You're welcome. https://t.co/nm3lew9kdd
Good time to remind everyone that Trump and RFK Jr stopped monitoring cyclospora last year
I cannot support a blank check for Trump's illegal war in Iran and fast-tracking deeply troubling and dangerous surveillance collaboration with Netanyahu’s government. I voted no.

The second murder by Trump's ICE thugs in a week.

Gunning down people in broad daylight doesn't make anyone safer.

It puts every single one of us at risk.

The Trump administration may start trucking radioactive Hanford nuclear waste through Oregon. Put me down as skeptical that Trump's minions can pull this off safely. I'm going to be fighting this like hell to prioritize the safety of Oregonians.
https://t.co/LIkDGt6i21

The Trump family will stop at nothing to enrich themselves at the expense of American taxpayers. 

Todd Blanche's role in orchestrating this unprecedented perversion of justice is one more reason why he is unfit to serve as Attorney General.
It seems Jay Clayton is up to his eyeballs in sending intimidation subpoenas to reporters and armed thugs to their homes. This is not acceptable in a DNI. https://t.co/bDYOOSS3u3

Happy 100th anniversary to this community treasure in NE Portland. 
https://t.co/UGcWV43Dt0
RT @CBSNews: Democratic Sens. Elizabeth Warren, Chuck Schumer and Ron Wyden are probing whether companies affiliated with the Trump family…

This is what democracy looks like. People of all ages, walks of life and zip codes coming together to find common ground and solutions to some of our state’s most urgent challenges like mental health and much more. https://t.co/c95jH1cgMT

Trump’s ICE shot and killed another person in broad daylight. 

They continue to terrorize our communities, stomp on our rights and make every single one of us less safe.

Few better ways before a #2026townhall than to spend a few minutes in one of Oregon’s many fabulous small bookstores. I was fortunate yesterday to have one of those moments in Wallowa County to browse in the fantastic Book Loft here in Enterprise. https://t.co/iBNMBhgTil

Americans pay the price at the gas pump, while Trump conducts foreign policy like a reality TV show.

You deserve better than this.

Wallowa County is almost 2,500 miles away from Washington, DC but the Oregonians who came to yesterday’s #2026townhall in Enterprise are very well-versed about the need to root out the corruption oozing out of the White House. https://t.co/4hE4pYWwcU

Very impressed by all I saw today in La Grande where students are learning trades while building homes the community needs. Glad to have worked with @SenJeffMerkley to secure the $515,000 federal investment to support this blueprint for success in Eastern Oregon. https://t.co/cOm4bIg5Ml

As always, Eastern Oregon citizens came well-prepared to today’s #2026townhall in La Grande. Thanks to the Union County residents who asked such smart & focused questions about forestry, the Epstein class, Big Insurance, major privacy abuses & more. https://t.co/x9075QwoMH

This is another desperate attempt by Trump to make it harder for Oregonians to vote this November because he hasn’t been successful in overturning vote by mail. I will fight this with everything I’ve got.

One of the best antidotes to big money in politics is a community meeting just like today’s excellent #2026townhall in Baker County where any Oregonian can ask any question & share any opinion. 

That’s how I’ve always done it & that’s how I’ll always keep doing it. https://t.co/5O5k0hYm59

I know I join with many here in Oregon and all over America honoring Navy Cmdr. Gabriel Edwards’ service to our country, and thinking of his wife and two children. This Oregon hero will not be forgotten. 

https://t.co/4MbzcBdxtG

A beautiful summer day here in John Day, which makes me all that more appreciative Grant County residents came in for today’s open-to-all #2026townhall to share their views & ask questions about the economy & health care here in Eastern Oregon. https://t.co/DW9CrOBcID

You shouldn't have to work two, three, or four jobs just to get by in America.

We need a $25/hr federal minimum wage. Period.

Thanks to Harney County for yesterday’s #2026townhall in Burns and a wonderful #OregonWay conversation about rural health care, vote-by-mail, White House corruption & much more. https://t.co/BoeAD0jTEK

Looks like I am not the only one who thinks this will be a special season for @oregonfootball.

Good to read here today at the Walgreen’s in Ontario that this preseason magazine has the team at the top. #GoDucks https://t.co/3PQ1csJXEB
It's time for my Billionaires Income Tax.

Great to be in Malheur County for today’s open-to-all #2026townhall — the first one right after our state & country celebrated the #FourthofJuly. I’ll be having five more town halls this week in Eastern Oregon to hear how Oregonians want to shape the next chapters of U.S. history https://t.co/MM3aIStVgy
My team and I have been working on all of this for a while and will be ready to roll out solid legislation once we can makes laws again (vote in November).
Disruption may be good for economic growth but disruptors should be paying SOMETHING for the dislocations they cause for communities, jobs, and industries.
And data centers need to stop competing with homeowners for power - new loads require new power - and not pollution spewing industrial generators in people’s backyards.

After America celebrates our country’s 250th birthday today on the #FourthofJuly & throughout the weekend, I’m looking forward to these open-to-all #2026townhalls in Eastern Oregon to kick off our country’s next chapter. https://t.co/FfdHi6AxC9

Today we celebrate 250 years of American freedom and liberty. 

Here's to the generations who poured their blood, sweat, and tears into defending those values.

Here’s to another 250 years of fighting just as hard to defend our democracy against those that seek to undermine it.
Trump continues to profit off of his presidency at unprecedented levels while many families across the country can barely afford to eat and pay rent.
https://t.co/q9wHnbSRUw
Everyone deserves the freedom to live without government interference in their personal lives. I will always fight to protect the rights of trans Americans.

Trump’s Supreme Court just opened the flood gate for billionaires and corporations to control America’s elections even more than they already do. The Court is hellbent on locking the American people out of their own democracy.
The 14th amendment is clear: if you are born in the United States, you are a U.S. citizen. The Constitution isn’t Donald Trump's to rewrite.

In light of this decision, the federal government should rethink if it can continue to buy Americans' location data without a warrant. I'll keep pushing for black-letter law that would stop the sale of your data to anyone, including the government.

Good news today that the law prevailed to stop one of Donald Trump’s unhinged & undemocratic schemes to mess with Oregon’s proven vote-by-mail system.
 
https://t.co/D0y1bUxlnN

RT @jacobbogage: 📬 This also has big potential mail-in voting consequences.

It could empower the president to fire members of the USPS boa…
This ruling is good news for democracy which means Trump will double down on his efforts to rig the November midterms

Well-deserved national honors for these talented students in Salem. Congrats to them and their teacher for their vision and work ethic.

https://t.co/9RVe4lZ0CA

The #OregonWay is all about getting out & listening to people throughout the community. After last week in Portland and before my upcoming open-to-all town halls in Eastern Oregon, I had a few thoughts on what I’ve been hearing. You can read more here:
https://t.co/nvBNjSCAmF

$25 an hour is the salary it takes to pay your bills, and what the minimum wage would be if it had kept pace with inflation. 

Proud to be leading the fight for a $25 minimum wage with @chrismurphyct.

Second, Black paid Epstein at rates that were 30 times higher than what he paid the world-class tax advisors he already employed. 

He didn’t need Epstein's advice, but he paid him like the CEO of a Fortune 100 company. 

Black's story has never passed the smell test

Two issues with this. 

First, Black's own attorneys told my investigators that Epstein's advice was shoddy at best and often needed double checking. 

Epstein was not a tax lawyer or accountant.

America’s health care system needs major surgery to make it affordable & accessible with Medicare-like choices. Here’s how you can help me to fix it.
https://t.co/BZObUxDiAT

SCOTUS decision slams the door on vulnerable people, including children, seeking refuge from persecution.

Asylum seekers come to the U.S. looking for a better life. The law says they deserve a fair shot.

The American Dream isn’t reserved for the rich and powerful.
Administrations like this are why we need surveillance reform.

Trump-flation is landing hard on older Americans served by community treasures like @MealsWheelsPpl 

https://t.co/HrbTsQWusn

Four years ago, Trump's Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade.

Today, he’s refusing to sign the first bipartisan housing bill in decades to pass his own voter suppression bill.

Trump is more concerned with restricting your freedoms than preserving them.

Trump and Pulte are showing the damage one unhinged President can do to America’s national security.

I’ve been fighting to protect rank and file intelligence professionals from political purges with new guardrails for years.

Republicans blocked every effort I made.
Every American should wonder why a seasoned and skilled political operator like Mr. Starmer would have named someone he knew to be associated with Jeffrey Epstein as Ambassador to the US.

He must have thought that an Epstein connection would benefit the Ambassador’s work with the current Administration and other American elites. That’s another reason why the Treasury’s Epstein files must be released to the American people.

Any nominee willing to be a part of Trump’s corruption of the IRS will face a lot of tough questions.

Should the Trump family be the ONLY family in America that's immune to IRS audits?

I guess we’re about to find out.

Hosting the #WorldCup now + the Olympics & Paralympics in 2028 adds up to a great opportunity for the U.S. economy & our global reputation. 

The Trump administration should treat it as such.
https://t.co/k9xMDnqDPF

Welcome to #RipCity, Coach Nori. You’re going to love it here. And I know @trailblazers fans are going to love watching you lead Dame, Deni & our whole team to lots of wins on the road and especially at home in the Moda Center.
 
https://t.co/qRy4Zzn8Tr
World’s first trillionaire and the minimum wage is still $7.25 an hour.

assessment.posture: No captured Kurdistan social posture yet
assessment.summary: The local archive has not captured a tweet from this account that matches TOR Phi's Kurdistan Lens terms yet. Capture more timeline pages and targeted Kurdistan searches before drawing a social-media conclusion.
assessment.whatItWasLike: No Kurdistan, Kurds, KRG/IKBY, Erbil, Peshmerga, Northern Iraq, PKK/YPG, Iraq/Syria, Yazidi/Sinjar, or energy-linked tweet is present in the profile window yet.

Saddened to hear @Oregonian reporter Zane Sparling has passed. Zane always came well-prepared with questions & a wonderfully quirky style that made him unique. My condolences to his colleagues & loved ones.
https://t.co/iWGHhbLpud

Over the weekend I celebrated the 54th Annual #Juneteenth Oregon Festival in Portland with longtime friends like Paul Knauls, colleagues and community partners from across the region. https://t.co/nZGiJ42JJx

So great to see @theportlandfire meeting the high expectations here in Oregon for fan enthusiasm that makes the Moda Center now the place to be for pro basketball all yearround.

https://t.co/FzwrSG1Ym8

Yesterday, I toured the new @sunshinedivision warehouse and food pantry here in Portland. While Trump and his cronies are set on leaving families in need hungry, these organizations are stepping up to keep Oregonians fed. https://t.co/zsO4DCCiD7

Glad to join Portlanders to kick off the World Refugee Day Walk. Immigrants and refugees make America better and stronger when they get a fair shake, and that’s been true for 250 years. https://t.co/zkGvjBAF3J

Great afternoon celebrating #Juneteenth today here in NE Portland with all the powerhouse small businesses gathered here at @mesopdx. Small businesses just like these are the backbone of Portland and our entire state. https://t.co/NoIoyUsp7n
Had a blast today here at Gol in SE Portland celebrating today’s big #WorldCup win by the @USMNT over Australia. https://t.co/unK9YcNbOz
Thanks to Jessica Maravilla of @ACLU_OR, state Senator @LewFrederick and state Reps. @RobNosse & Lamar Wise for standing up with me on this essential civil liberties question.

Proud to stand in Portland this #Juneteenth and #PrideMonth with the ACLU, state Black & LGBTQ+ legislators to say today & everyday we’re not going backward to the awful days when the government weaponized surveillance against these American groups & so many others. https://t.co/P1KfMXvXN8

Very much enjoyed beginning this #Juneteenth here in Portland today at the interfaith clergy breakfast with the Coalition of African and African American Pastors/Imams & Bridge Pajoma - two groups that both do so much for our city. https://t.co/rQbramXo6p

More @theportlandfire games at the Moda Center? Count me in.
https://t.co/8qEVKUdvwh

With the first day of summer still ahead, soaring gas prices from Donald Trump’s Iran war are driving up firefighting costs for Oregon communities at the worst possible time.

https://t.co/hKnBb4VKDM

Oregon’s world-class agriculture scores again.

https://t.co/eJdgvlPUc1

Millions of Americans are struggling to afford gas, groceries, and health care while Trump spends your taxpayer dollars on stuff like this:

$300 million for a ballroom

$14 million for a reflecting pool renovation

$50 billion+ for a war in Iran

In the 14 years since DACA was established, Dreamers have made countless contributions, working hard to better their country and communities.

I will always fight to protect the lives and livelihoods of Dreamers that call America home.

Donald Trump asked visitors to natural & historic treasures in Oregon and nationwide to report any examples of so-called “negative” information. 

Here’s what they actually said:
https://t.co/fR6Yre7fp7

RT @fox12oregon: Multnomah County opens two cooling center for heat wave

https://t.co/ctfzcjYcwJ
There’s no debate that these Portland-area students are a forensics force. https://t.co/2DW81MggfV
RT @trimet: TriMet won't turn away anyone riding to or from a cooling center who cannot pay fare between 11 a.m. and 10 p.m. today, Monday,…

Big @OregonTF wins for Aaliyah McCormick in the 100-meter hurdles & Simeon Birnbaum in the 1,500 meters at the #NCAATF in Eugene. Huge congrats to both for their titles & adding to the school’s track-and-field trophy case. #GoDucks

The swastikas & other hate symbols detailed in this piece are awful.  But the community response in Washington County is inspiring.
 
https://t.co/3oumczft4n

No matter WHO is DNI, no government official should be able to spy on Americans without a warrant.

Reforming Section 702 is the only path forward.

https://t.co/efBobjOPHr

It doesn’t matter who’s in charge. 

FISA 702 can’t be renewed without real reforms. 

Case in point: Trump’s latest nominee for director of national intelligence was peddling election conspiracies just a few days ago.
Republicans just blocked a five week FISA extension in order to cover up ongoing FISA abuses.
The senator from Texas seems just as confused about my FISA reform bill as he is about what state I've represented for many years

RT @SenateFinance: Inflation is at its highest level in 3 years.

This is Trump's economy.

Inflation is at its highest point in 3 years because of Trump's illegal tariffs and war in Iran.

That's "Trumpenomics" for you...

The Senate is set to vote on renewing Trump’s spying powers this week.

If section 702 of FISA is renewed, people like Bill Pulte, Todd Blanche, and Kash Patel will be able to invade any American's privacy.

I’m going to fight like Hell to make sure that doesn’t happen.
RT @CalltoActivism: 🚨ALARMING: Sen. Ron Wyden has introduced legislation to STOP Trump and Patel from gaining access to INNOCENT Americans'…

RT @CalltoActivism: 🚨COLOSSAL NATIONAL SECURITY BREACH

Sen. Ron Wyden says foreign adversaries were able to access the LOCATION DATA of Am…
RT @CalltoActivism: 🚨HELL YEAH: Senate Finance Committee Ranking Member Ron Wyden just dropped the gauntlet on Trump’s slush fund after exp…
Gutting $1 trillion from Medicaid wasnt enough Republicans are coming for your Social Security and Medicare

Reminder that Trump’s Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is sitting on Epstein's financial records showing 4,700+ wire transfers totaling over $1.1 BILLION in and out of his accounts, including payments to women and girls. 

The American people deserve full transparency.

We have never seen a coalition like the one that stood up against the plan to rubber-stamp this spying authority. Elected officials from both sides of the aisle are waking up to the fact that *no president* should have this power.

I'll be fighting like hell between now and June 12 to ensure Congress doesn't cave and renew Section 702 of FISA without real reforms. Security and liberty aren't mutually exclusive, and it seems like Congress is finally starting to understand that.

An update on where things stand on FISA:

Early Friday morning, we successfully defeated a bill in the Senate to rubber-stamp the Trump administration's authority to spy on Americans without a warrant. This is a big win on privacy.
